Event: Frodo accidentally causes Gollum and the Ring to fall into the lava while trying to take the Ring back for himself
Explanation: Though selfishly motivated and injurious to Gollum, his struggle results in the Ring’s destruction and Sauron’s fall, hugely benefiting others and ultimately freeing him.

Event: Frodo fights back despite being less combat-capable and survives his ordeals
Explanation: His limited but real fighting efforts keep him alive and contribute to the quest’s progress.

Event: Frodo escapes Shelob's lair
Explanation: Survival and escape preserve the Ringbearer and thus the quest, indirectly helping Middle-earth.

Event: Frodo is stabbed by the Witch-King, later healed by elves
Explanation: The stab nearly destroys him, but elven healing saves his life at great effort; others bear cost to preserve him and the quest.

Event: The Ring’s influence leads Frodo to alienate Sam
Explanation: He harms his closest ally and damages their bond without gaining any durable benefit.

Event: Frodo leaves for the Undying Lands with Gandalf because he cannot recover from his ordeal
Explanation: He seeks healing and peace while bequeathing the Shire to Sam and others, giving closure all around.

Event: Bilbo gives Frodo the Ring, making him its inheritor
Explanation: Frodo takes on immense danger and trauma while Middle-earth eventually gains salvation through his bearing of the Ring.

Event: Gandalf tells Frodo he was meant to have the Ring, encouraging him
Explanation: His counsel strengthens Frodo’s resolve, aiding both Frodo’s purpose and the world’s hopes.

Event: Bilbo gives Sting to Frodo
Explanation: The gift arms Frodo with a useful weapon, helping his survival and the quest.

Event: Frodo rescues himself from Boromir by putting on the Ring and shoving Boromir away
Explanation: He protects himself and escapes while briefly worsening Boromir’s corruption/obsession.

Event: Frodo threatens Gollum with Sting when Gollum ambushes and he nearly chokes Sam to death
Explanation: His aggressive defense deters Gollum but also endangers Sam and reflects Ring-fueled volatility.

Event: Frodo orders Sam to beat him up while disguised as orcs to create a distraction
Explanation: He and Sam incur some short-term pain but the ploy diverts suspicion and protects their cover.

Event: Frodo solves the riddle in Moria after Gandalf fails
Explanation: His insight gets them into Moria safely and affirms his value to the group.

Event: Bilbo adopts Frodo and later makes him heir to Bag End
Explanation: Frodo gains a loving home and inheritance while Bilbo gains a devoted heir and companion.

Event: Frodo goes silent after Gandalf falls in Moria
Explanation: His emotional withdrawal offers no clear benefit and leaves others without his leadership or comfort.

Event: Frodo perseveres for years under the Ring’s influence, bringing it almost to destruction
Explanation: He suffers enormous personal cost but nearly completes the task no other mortal could, enabling the Ring’s eventual destruction.

Event: Frodo sets off from the Shire with the Ring instead of going home when offered the choice
Explanation: He sacrifices safety and normalcy to carry the Ring toward Mount Doom, crucially advancing the quest.

Event: Frodo leaves Middle-earth for the Undying Lands as a reward after being unable to recover
Explanation: He finds hoped-for healing while allowing the new age to proceed without an ailing Ringbearer.

Event: Sam and Frodo disguise themselves as orcs to travel through hostile territory
Explanation: The ruse protects them and advances their mission while avoiding needless fighting and casualties.

Event: Sam rescues Frodo from Cirith Ungol and they don orc armor to pass the army unharmed
Explanation: Sam saves Frodo and uses a clever disguise to bypass enemies, benefiting them and minimizing wider bloodshed.

Event: Frodo laments that he wishes the Ring had never come to him
Explanation: His despair harms his own morale with no real gain, though it elicits helpful counsel from Gandalf.

Event: Frodo attempts to abandon the rest of the Fellowship so he doesn’t drag them into danger
Explanation: He sacrifices his own safety and support network to spare his friends additional peril.

Event: Frodo refuses to let Sam carry the Ring, insisting it would destroy Sam
Explanation: He keeps lethal risk on himself to protect Sam, bearing more harm while preserving his friend.

Event: Frodo decides to send Sam away at Cirith Ungol
Explanation: He loses his strongest ally and devastates Sam, making both their situations worse with no benefit.

Event: Frodo later realizes sending Sam away was a horrible mistake
Explanation: His realization brings guilt but prompts partial emotional repair once Sam returns, indirectly helping their relationship.

Event: Frodo is betrayed by Gollum in Shelob’s cave after trusting him
Explanation: Frodo is gravely endangered as a result of his mercy, but Gollum’s role ultimately aids the Ring’s destruction.

Event: Frodo rejects killing Gollum and shows him mercy despite his past threats
Explanation: He risks his own safety by sparing Gollum, whose survival later proves essential to the Ring’s destruction.

Event: Frodo hopes to redeem Gollum and tries to draw out his former good self
Explanation: His effort benefits Gollum somewhat and moderates cruelty, but increases personal risk and fails long-term.

Event: Frodo pushing Sam toward Rosie and making him dance with her at Bilbo’s party
Explanation: His meddling helps Sam and Rosie connect romantically and gives Frodo social satisfaction.

Event: Frodo forces Sam to dance with Rosie when Sam is too shy
Explanation: Sam is embarrassed briefly but gains a relationship that leads to marriage and happiness.

Event: Frodo snarks about Rosie knowing an idiot when she sees one
Explanation: His teasing reinforces camaraderie and comfort between them with no real harm.

Event: Frodo keeps going because of Sam’s loyalty and support
Explanation: Sam’s steadfastness sustains Frodo and enables the Ring’s destruction, hugely benefiting all.

Event: Frodo channels frightening, feral expressions and snarls at Sam during a fight under the Ring’s influence
Explanation: He emotionally and physically endangers Sam while undermining their trust, gaining nothing lasting.

Event: Frodo stares at Sam with a Kubrick Stare before putting on the Ring and vanishing
Explanation: His menacing look and disappearance alarm Sam and mark his submission to the Ring without benefiting him.

Event: Frodo acts as leader of the other hobbits while fleeing the Ringwraiths
Explanation: His leadership organizes their flight and increases everyone’s chance of survival.

Event: Frodo attempts to abandon all the rest of the Fellowship again so he doesn’t endanger them
Explanation: He reduces danger to others by isolating himself, but severely harms his own odds.

Event: Frodo refuses to let Sam carry the Ring a second time, fearing it would destroy Sam
Explanation: He retains a deadly burden to shield Sam, worsening his own condition while preserving Sam’s.

Event: Frodo threatens to cut Gollum’s throat to make him release Sam
Explanation: He uses forceful threat to protect Sam and reassert control, serving their safety despite harshness.

Event: Frodo treats Gollum with increasing mercy and trust, which becomes key to fulfilling the Quest
Explanation: His mercy heightens his personal risk yet ensures Gollum’s role in the Ring’s eventual destruction.

Event: Frodo wears the Ring on a chain around his neck instead of in his pocket
Explanation: He improves physical security and reduces accidental use, despite discomfort and injury.

Event: Orcs strip Frodo’s mithril vest to send it to Sauron as proof of his death
Explanation: They harm and humiliate Frodo and mislead the Fellowship while gaining favor and information for Sauron.

Event: Frodo comes up with an on-the-spot plan so he and Sam won’t be caught by orcs
Explanation: His improvisation protects them and furthers the mission with minimal collateral harm.

Event: Frodo grows more angry and paranoid the longer he bears the Ring
Explanation: His worsening temper strains relationships and undermines support without helping the quest.

Event: Frodo takes on the Ring’s long-term psychological damage and accepts that he cannot go home
Explanation: He endures permanent trauma while his sacrifice underpins the world’s peace.

Event: Frodo realizes he is irreversibly changed and chooses to leave Middle-earth forever
Explanation: He seeks healing in the West and allows his friends and homeland to flourish without his unresolved wounds.
